summary: Comprehensive testing of blog edit fix completed successfully. All requested features working: Blog edit loads with pre-filled data, blog create works, testimonial edit/create works, no runtime errors on blog editor, public blog pages work correctly.

issues: Non-boolean attribute warnings for 'jsx' and 'global' attributes in Quill editor - cosmetic only, does not affect functionality

seed_data_creation: Test data created during backend tests (TEST: prefixed posts and testimonials)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All blog edit functionality verified working. BlogEditor uses vanilla Quill (not ReactQuill) to avoid React 19 concurrent rendering issues. Routes /admin/blog/edit/:postId and /admin/testimonials/edit/:testimonialId are properly configured in App.js.

features_tested.blog_edit_click_from_dashboard: PASS - Edit button on dashboard navigates to /admin/blog/edit/:postId
features_tested.blog_edit_loads_existing_data: PASS - BlogEditor loads with title, excerpt, content pre-filled from existing post
features_tested.blog_edit_modify_and_submit: PASS - Modified title saved successfully, redirects to dashboard, changes visible
features_tested.blog_create_new_post: PASS - /admin/blog/new loads empty form with 'Nouvel article de blog' title
features_tested.blog_create_share_checkbox: PASS - Share to social checkbox visible only on new posts
features_tested.testimonial_edit_loads_existing_data: PASS - TestimonialEditor loads with name, text, rating pre-filled
features_tested.testimonial_create_new: PASS - /admin/testimonials/new loads empty form with 'Nouveau temoignage' title
features_tested.no_runtime_error_overlay: PASS - No error overlay on any blog editor page (create or edit)
features_tested.blog_listing_page: PASS - /blog shows published posts without errors
features_tested.quill_editor_visible: PASS - Vanilla Quill toolbar visible and functional on blog editor

bug_fix_verification.blog_editor_edit_mode: VERIFIED - BlogEditor supports both create and edit modes via useParams
bug_fix_verification.vanilla_quill_implementation: VERIFIED - Using vanilla Quill via dynamic import instead of ReactQuill to avoid React 19 concurrent rendering errors
